text.title: java-spring-boot-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-spring-boot-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Anemic domain model: entity has only getters/setters, business logic lives in service.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Domain invariants (e.g., "can't promote if already admin") cannot be enforced; any service can bypass them.

failure-mode.description: N+1 query: iterating a list and accessing a lazy association inside the loop.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Produces one query per item. Fix: JOIN FETCH in @Query or @EntityGraph on the repository method.

failure-mode.description: @Transactional on a controller method or on a private service method.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Spring AOP proxy cannot intercept private methods or the controller layer; the annotation is silently ignored.
